solve(Nums, Queries) ->
    % Precompute popcount-depth for all possible numbers up to 1e15
    % But since numbers can be up to 1e15, we'll compute on the fly with memoization
    % However, in practice, we can memoize the depth for numbers encountered
    % Initialize a map to store precomputed depths
    % For each query, we'll process it and maintain the current nums and a memo of depths
    
    % Process queries one by one
    process_queries(Nums, Queries, [], #{}).

process_queries(Nums, [], Answers, _Memo) ->
    lists:reverse(Answers);
process_queries(Nums, [Query | Rest], Answers, Memo) ->
    case Query of
        [1, L, R, K] ->
            % Count elements in [L, R] with depth K
            {Count, NewMemo} = count_in_range(Nums, L, R, K, Memo),
            process_queries(Nums, Rest, [Count | Answers], NewMemo);
        [2, Idx, Val] ->
            % Update nums[Idx] to Val
            NewNums = set_nth(Idx + 1, Val, Nums),
            process_queries(NewNums, Rest, Answers, Memo)
    end.

count_in_range(Nums, L, R, K, Memo) ->
    SubList = lists:sublist(Nums, L + 1, R - L + 1),
    {Count, NewMemo} = lists:foldl(
        fun(Num, {Cnt, M}) ->
            {Depth, M1} = get_depth(Num, M),
            if Depth =:= K -> {Cnt + 1, M1};
               true -> {Cnt, M1}
            end
        end,
        {0, Memo},
        SubList
    ),
    {Count, NewMemo}.

get_depth(Num, Memo) ->
    case maps:get(Num, Memo, undefined) of
        undefined ->
            Depth = compute_depth(Num),
            {Depth, maps:put(Num, Depth, Memo)};
        Depth ->
            {Depth, Memo}
    end.

compute_depth(Num) ->
    compute_depth(Num, 0).

compute_depth(1, D) -> D;
compute_depth(Num, D) ->
    Next = popcount(Num),
    compute_depth(Next, D + 1).

popcount(N) ->
    popcount(N, 0).

popcount(0, Count) -> Count;
popcount(N, Count) ->
    popcount(N bsr 1, Count + (N band 1)).

set_nth(1, Val, [_ | T]) -> [Val | T];
set_nth(N, Val, [H | T]) -> [H | set_nth(N - 1, Val, T)].

solve(Nums, Queries) ->
    % Precompute popcount-depth for all possible nums
    DepthMap = build_depth_map(),
    
    % Initialize the state with the initial nums and their depths
    InitialDepths = [maps:get(X, DepthMap) || X <- Nums],
    {_, Answers} = lists:foldl(
        fun(Query, {CurrentNums, CurrentDepths, Acc}) ->
            case Query of
                [1, L, R, K] ->
                    Count = count_in_range(CurrentDepths, L, R, K),
                    {CurrentNums, CurrentDepths, [Count | Acc]};
                [2, Idx, Val] ->
                    NewDepth = maps:get(Val, DepthMap),
                    NewNums = set_nth(Idx + 1, Val, CurrentNums),
                    NewDepths = set_nth(Idx + 1, NewDepth, CurrentDepths),
                    {NewNums, NewDepths, Acc}
            end
        end,
        {Nums, InitialDepths, []},
        lists:reverse(Queries)
    ),
    lists:reverse(Answers).

build_depth_map() ->
    MaxNum = 1 bsl 60, % A large enough number to cover 1e15
    DepthMap = maps:new(),
    build_depth_map(1, MaxNum, DepthMap).

build_depth_map(X, MaxNum, Map) when X > MaxNum ->
    Map;
build_depth_map(X, MaxNum, Map) ->
    Depth = compute_depth(X),
    NewMap = maps:put(X, Depth, Map),
    build_depth_map(X + 1, MaxNum, NewMap).

compute_depth(X) ->
    compute_depth(X, 0).

compute_depth(1, D) ->
    D;
compute_depth(X, D) ->
    PopCount = popcount(X),
    compute_depth(PopCount, D + 1).

popcount(X) ->
    popcount(X, 0).

popcount(0, Count) ->
    Count;
popcount(X, Count) ->
    popcount(X bsr 1, Count + (X band 1)).

count_in_range(Depths, L, R, K) ->
    Sublist = lists:sublist(Depths, L + 1, R - L + 1),
    length([D || D <- Sublist, D =:= K]).

set_nth(1, Val, [_ | T]) -> [Val | T];
set_nth(N, Val, [H | T]) -> [H | set_nth(N - 1, Val, T)].
